title: Coordinate Systems Lab: Project and Transform
description: Use QGIS to load the provided shapefile in WGS 84 geographic coordinates and reproject it to UTM Zone 4N (EPSG:32604). Verify coordinates before and after transformation and export a PDF map with a proper scale bar and north arrow.

title: Attribute Table Queries and Joins
description: Load the Hawaiian reef habitat vector layer and perform three attribute queries using the QGIS expression builder: (1) select coral-dominated polygons above 5 hectares, (2) join species count data from the CSV, (3) export a filtered layer to GeoJSON.

title: CRS Quiz: Geographic vs Projected
description: What is the key difference between a geographic coordinate system and a projected coordinate system? Give one example where using the wrong CRS would produce a significant measurement error in a marine habitat analysis.

title: DEM Analysis: Slope and Bathymetry
description: Load the provided bathymetric DEM of Kaneohe Bay. Calculate slope and aspect rasters using QGIS terrain analysis tools. Identify and annotate three zones of steep reef drop-offs relevant to coral survey planning. Export as a labeled map.

title: Georeferencing a Historical Reef Chart
description: Georeference the scanned 1978 NOAA reef chart of Kaneohe Bay using at least 10 ground control points tied to current satellite imagery. Report RMS error and describe any spatial distortions you observe between the 1978 chart and present-day basemap.

title: Spatial Query: Buffer and Intersect
description: Using the reef monitoring sites point layer, create 200m buffers around each site, then intersect with the human-impact polygons layer (boat moorings and runoff zones). Quantify the proportion of each buffer area affected by each impact type.

title: Map Design Project: Reef Health Overview
description: Design a publication-quality map showing reef health index scores across Kaneohe Bay monitoring sites. Apply a diverging color scheme from the course color-theory guidelines, add graticule labels, inset locator map, data sources, and a clear legend.

title: GPS Field Data Collection Exercise
description: Record at least 15 waypoints using a GPS-enabled device during your next field outing. Import the GPX file into QGIS, snap points to the base reef polygon, verify positional accuracy against satellite imagery, and produce a field data collection summary map.

title: Module 1 Final: Integrated GIS Analysis
description: Produce a complete GIS analysis workflow document: load raw data, reproject, clean attributes, perform two geoprocessing operations (buffer + union), and deliver a final map with written methods paragraph (250 words max). Submit both QGIS project file (.qgz) and exported PDF.

title: Vector Overlay: Clip, Intersect, and Difference
description: Using QGIS perform clip intersect and difference overlay operations on the provided marine protected area boundaries and reef habitat polygons. Document how each tool changes the geometry and attribute count. Submit a before-and-after map and a 200-word reflection.

title: Raster Suitability Model: Reef Survey Site Selection
description: Build a weighted raster suitability model to identify optimal reef survey locations. Input layers: bathymetry slope SST anomaly and boat-access distance. Assign weights justify your choices and map the top-10 candidate survey cells.

title: Spatial Autocorrelation: Moran's I on Coral Cover
description: Load the coral cover percentage point dataset. Compute Moran's I statistic in QGIS or R to test for spatial clustering. Produce a LISA cluster map identify hotspots and coldspots and interpret the ecological significance in a short paragraph.

title: R Spatial Lab: sf and terra Workflow
description: Complete the provided R Markdown template using the sf and terra packages: load a reef survey GeoPackage, compute fish species richness per transect polygon, join to spatial object, and produce a choropleth map using ggplot2 and tmap.

title: Least-Cost Path: Diver Transit Routes
description: Using the bathymetric surface as a cost raster model least-cost diver transit routes between three paired survey stations in Kaneohe Bay. Compare cumulative cost against straight-line distance and discuss implications for survey planning.

title: Marine Spatial Planning Case Study
description: Select a real-world marine spatial planning scenario (e.g. MPA boundary design trawl exclusion zone or reef restoration site). Apply at least two spatial analysis techniques from the course to evaluate the scenario and present findings as a 2-page spatial analysis report with maps.

title: Module 2 Final: Spatial Analysis Portfolio
description: Compile your best work from Module 2 into a spatial analysis portfolio. Include three maps from previous assignments with revised figure captions an updated methods narrative and a one-page reflection on how spatial analysis tools apply to your professional research context.

title: Spectral Signatures Worksheet
description: Plot spectral reflectance curves for five land/water cover types (coral reef clear water turbid water seagrass bare sand) using the provided Sentinel-2 band values. Label peaks and troughs and explain which bands best discriminate coral from surrounding habitat.

title: NDVI vs NDWI Quiz
description: Explain the difference between NDVI and NDWI. Which index would you apply to identify submerged reef structures from Landsat 8 imagery of a shallow coastal bay, and why?

title: Supervised Classification: Coral Habitat Map
description: Using the provided Sentinel-2 tile of Kaneohe Bay perform a supervised maximum likelihood classification into four classes: coral reef seagrass algal turf and open water. Assess accuracy using the provided validation points and report overall accuracy and kappa coefficient.

title: Change Detection: Reef Bleaching Analysis
description: Write a 2-page analysis of coral bleaching extent using before-and-after Landsat imagery from a bleaching event year of your choice. Apply image differencing or band ratio change detection. Map bleaching extent and cite at least 3 peer-reviewed sources to contextualize your findings.

title: Drone Survey Planning Presentation
description: Create a 5-minute presentation outlining a drone survey design for a reef monitoring project. Include flight altitude GSD calculation overlap settings sensor selection and data processing pipeline. Discuss tradeoffs between spatial resolution and area coverage.

title: Capstone Data Upload: Reef Transect Waypoints
description: Upload all GPS waypoints from your reef transect surveys in GPX and CSV format. Label each file by site code and date (e.g. KB-T1-20260511.gpx). Include RAW coordinates and field notes for each waypoint.

title: QGIS Installation Verification
description: Install QGIS 3.34 LTR on your machine and confirm successful installation by submitting a screenshot showing the QGIS startup screen with version number visible. Also confirm R 4.4+ is installed and the sf and terra packages load without errors.

title: Data Repository Access Check
description: Log in to the Pacific Environmental GIS Certificate data portal and download the orientation sample dataset (kaneohe_sample_v1.gpkg). Confirm the file opens in QGIS and displays the three expected layers: reef polygons monitoring points and bathymetry contours.

title: Cohort Intro Survey
description: Complete the 10-question cohort introductory survey. Questions cover your GIS background professional context goals for the certification and preferred meeting times for optional live sessions.

title: Certification Pathway Agreement
description: Review the certification requirements document (mat_007) and submit the signed pathway agreement form confirming you understand module sequence assessment policies late-submission rules and the December 2026 final capstone deadline.

title: Peer Introduction Post
description: Post a brief introduction (150 words max) in the cohort discussion board: your professional role primary geographic area of work one GIS skill you already have and one you are most excited to develop. Respond to at least two classmates.

title: R and QGIS Plugin Environment Check
description: Install required QGIS plugins (SAGA Processing gdal2xyz) and R packages (sf terra tmap ggplot2 spdep). Submit a screenshot of your R session info output confirming package versions.

title: Data Format Literacy Quiz
description: Match each file format (Shapefile GeoPackage GeoTIFF GeoJSON KML GPX) to its primary use case. Identify which formats support multiple layers within a single file and which are best suited for web-based delivery.

title: EPSG Code Lookup Exercise
description: Look up and record the EPSG codes for: WGS 84 geographic, Hawaii State Plane (NAD83), UTM Zone 4N, and Web Mercator. For each explain when you would choose it for a marine conservation project in Hawaii.

title: Goal-Setting Reflection
description: Write a 200-word reflection on your personal goals for this GIS certification: which modules you anticipate finding most challenging what professional outcomes you hope to achieve by December 2026 and how GIS skills will support your current research or work.

title: Sample Dataset Exploration
description: Open the kaneohe_sample_v1.gpkg in QGIS. Identify the CRS of each layer, check the number of features, and describe one spatial pattern you notice. Submit a screenshot of your QGIS canvas with all three layers styled and visible.

title: Cohort Schedule Acknowledgment
description: Review the 2026 cohort module schedule and confirm your availability for all synchronous review sessions. Note any scheduling conflicts in the submission comment field.

title: Open-Source GIS Literature Review Starter
description: Find and annotate two peer-reviewed articles that use open-source GIS tools (QGIS GRASS or R spatial packages) in marine or coastal conservation research. Submit citations in APA format with a 3-sentence summary of each paper's spatial methodology.

title: Remote Sensing Data Portal Access
description: Create accounts on USGS EarthExplorer and Copernicus Open Access Hub. Download one Sentinel-2 L2A tile and one Landsat 9 scene over Hawaii. Submit a screenshot showing successful download and file size for each.

title: Capstone Topic Proposal (Early Draft)
description: Submit a one-paragraph early draft of your capstone project topic. Describe the geographic focus data you expect to use and the spatial analysis methods you plan to apply. This is ungraded feedback only — revisions expected before final proposal in Module 5.

title: GIS Ethics and Indigenous Data Sovereignty Reading
description: Read the provided excerpt from the CARE Principles for Indigenous Data Governance (Carroll et al. 2020). Write a 150-word reflection on how these principles apply when using traditional ecological knowledge or community-collected reef data in a GIS project.

title: Metadata Standards Overview
description: Summarize the key fields required by the ISO 19115 geospatial metadata standard. Create a filled-in metadata template for one of your existing spatial datasets using the provided ISO 19115 form.

title: Version Control for GIS Projects
description: Set up a GitHub repository for your GIS certification work. Initialize with a README describing your project directory structure. Push a test QGIS project file and one sample shapefile. Submit the repository URL.

title: Orientation Completion Confirmation
description: Confirm completion of all orientation tasks by submitting this final checklist item. Acknowledge that you have: installed software, accessed the data portal, reviewed the certification requirements, and posted your peer introduction.

title: QGIS Installation Verification (2025 Cohort)
description: Install QGIS 3.28 LTR on your machine and confirm successful installation by submitting a screenshot showing the QGIS startup screen with version number visible.

title: Data Portal Access Check (2025 Cohort)
description: Log in to the Pacific Environmental GIS Certificate data portal and download the 2025 sample dataset. Confirm the file opens in QGIS.

title: Cohort Intro Survey (2025 Cohort)
description: Complete the 2025 cohort introductory survey covering GIS background professional context and goals for the certification.

title: Certification Pathway Agreement (2025 Cohort)
description: Review the 2025 certification requirements and submit the signed pathway agreement form.

title: Peer Introduction Post (2025 Cohort)
description: Post a brief introduction in the 2025 cohort discussion board: your professional role GIS experience and one goal for the year.

title: R and QGIS Plugin Environment Check (2025 Cohort)
description: Install required QGIS plugins and R packages for 2025 curriculum. Submit screenshot of session info.

title: Data Format Literacy Quiz (2025 Cohort)
description: Match each GIS file format to its primary use case from the 2025 orientation list.

title: EPSG Code Lookup Exercise (2025 Cohort)
description: Look up EPSG codes for the four standard CRSs used in Pacific marine conservation projects and explain each one's use case.

title: Goal-Setting Reflection (2025 Cohort)
description: Write a 200-word reflection on your personal goals for the 2025 GIS certification cohort.

title: Sample Dataset Exploration (2025 Cohort)
description: Open the 2025 sample GeoPackage in QGIS. Identify the CRS of each layer, check the number of features, and describe one spatial pattern. Submit a screenshot.

title: Cohort Schedule Acknowledgment (2025 Cohort)
description: Review the 2025 cohort module schedule and confirm your availability for synchronous review sessions.

title: Open-Source GIS Literature Review Starter (2025 Cohort)
description: Find and annotate two peer-reviewed articles using open-source GIS in marine or coastal conservation. Submit citations and 3-sentence summaries.

title: Remote Sensing Data Portal Access (2025 Cohort)
description: Create accounts on USGS EarthExplorer and Copernicus Open Access Hub. Download one Sentinel-2 tile over Hawaii. Submit a screenshot showing successful download.

title: Capstone Topic Proposal (Early Draft, 2025 Cohort)
description: Submit a one-paragraph early draft of your capstone project topic for the 2025 cohort.

title: GIS Ethics and Indigenous Data Sovereignty Reading (2025 Cohort)
description: Read the provided CARE Principles excerpt and write a 150-word reflection on applying these principles to reef GIS data.

title: Metadata Standards Overview (2025 Cohort)
description: Summarize key ISO 19115 fields and create a metadata template for one of your existing spatial datasets.

title: Version Control for GIS Projects (2025 Cohort)
description: Set up a GitHub repository for your 2025 GIS certification work. Submit the repository URL.

title: Orientation Completion Confirmation (2025 Cohort)
description: Confirm completion of all 2025 orientation tasks by submitting this final checklist item.
